metadata.title: High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero

beats.narration_text: Sawubona. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of high-d sphere volume: peaks at n=5, then shrinks to zero. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of high-d sphere volume: peaks at n=5, then shrinks to zero
output: ✓ High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero

beats.narration_text: High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Unit sphere volume peaks at n=5 (V₅ ≈ 5.264), then collapses toward zero. In high dimensions, the sphere is negligible inside its own bounding box.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Dimension n (cursor). Verify: Unit sphere volume peaks at n=5 (V₅ ≈ 5.264), then collapses toward zero. In high dimensions, the sphere is negligible i. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: high-d sphere volume: peaks at n=5, then shrinks to zero. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Dimension n (cursor) from 0 to 25. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Dimension n (cursor) from 0 to 25

beats.narration_text: Unit sphere volume peaks at n=5 (V₅ ≈ 5.264), then collapses toward zero. In high dimensions, the sphere is negligible inside its own bounding box.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of high-d sphere volume: peaks at n=5, then shrinks to zero. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this High-D Sphere Volume: Peaks at n=5, Then Shrinks to Zero simulation and add a second slider that controls Dimension n (cursor). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
